title: Yale University Hematoxylin and Eosin (H&E) Staining for Formalin-Fixed Paraffin-Embedded Human Lymph Node Tissues Protocol Adapted from VHIR-CIBERNED-ASAP
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7g3he","text":"Hematoxylin and Eosin (H\u0026E) Staining for for Formalin-Fixed Paraffin-Embedded Human Lymph Node Tissues after sectioning.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Bake the tissue section on the slide at 60 o C - 1 hr
Immerse the slide in 100% alcohol_1 - 6 quick immersions
Immerse the slide in Xylene_2 - 6 quick immersions
Immerse the slide in 70% alcohol - 6 quick immersions
Immerse the slide in 96% alcohol - 6 quick immersions
Immerse the slide in 100% alcohol_2 - 6 quick immersions
Immerse the slide in 50% alcohol - 6 quick immersions
Immerse the slide in Distilled water (H₂O) - 2 minutes
Apply Harris Hematoxylin - according to preparation
Run the slide under tap water (H₂O) - 5 minutes
Apply Acid alcohol - 2 minutes. Acid alcohol is made by adding 0.3 ml of HCl to 250 ml of 70º alcohol.
Run the slide under tap water (H₂O) - 5 minutes
Apply NaHCO₃ - 1 minute. Add one tablespoon of NaHCO₃ to 250 ml of distilled water (H₂O).
Run the slide under tap water (H₂O) - 5 minutes
Immerse the slide in 96% alcohol - 3 minutes.
Apply Eosin - according to preparation
Immerse the slide in 96% alcohol - 6 quick immersions
Immerse the slide in 96% alcohol - 6 quick immersions
Immerse the slide in 100% alcohol - 6 quick immersions
Immerse the slide in 100% alcohol - 6 quick immersions
Immerse the slide in Xylene - until the preparations are clear. Leave in xylene until mounting.
Immerse the slide in Xylene_1 - 5 minutes
creator: Negin Farzad
